steps.prompt: Is there a finite verb in this sentence?
steps.target: No — this is a noun phrase (the verb comes later in the poem)
choices: No — this is a noun phrase (the verb comes later in the poem)

hints.Yes — 'Arma' is a verb: 'Arma' is a neuter plural noun meaning 'arms/weapons'
hints.Yes — 'gravi' is a verb: 'gravi' is an adjective in the ablative case meaning 'heavy'
hints.Yes — 'bella' is a verb: 'bella' is a neuter plural noun meaning 'wars' (not from 'bellare')

hints.Nominative: While 'arma' could be nominative plural neuter, here it is the object echoing Vergil's opening
hints.Genitive: 'arma' has no genitive form ending in -a — genitive plural would be 'armorum'
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural of 'arma' would be 'armis' — 'arma' is nominative or accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive plural in -arum — 'armorum' ends in -orum
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive plural in -um/-ium — 'armorum' ends in -orum
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive plural in -uum — 'armorum' ends in -orum

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'gravis' is 'gravis' — 'gravi' has the -i ablative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'gravis' is 'gravis' — 'gravi' has the -i ablative ending for 3rd decl. adjectives
hints.Dative: Dative of 'gravis' would also be 'gravi', but here it modifies ablative 'numero'

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'numerus' is 'numerus' — 'numero' has the -o ablative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'numerus' would be 'numeri' — 'numero' ends in -o
hints.Dative: While dative of 'numerus' is also 'numero', the context of 'in heavy meter' indicates ablative of means

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'numeri' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'numeri' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'numeri' ends in -i

hints.or: 'or' in Latin is 'vel' or 'aut' — '-que' is an enclitic meaning 'and'
hints.but: 'but' in Latin is 'sed' or 'autem' — '-que' is an enclitic meaning 'and'
hints.nor: 'nor' in Latin is 'neque' or 'nec' — '-que' is an enclitic meaning 'and'

hints.Nominative: While 'bella' could be nominative neuter plural, here it parallels 'arma' as an object
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'bellum' would be 'belli' (sg.) or 'bellorum' (pl.) — 'bella' is nom./acc. plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural of 'bellum' would be 'bellis' — 'bella' is the accusative plural neuter

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'belli' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'belli' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'belli' ends in -i

translation: Arms in heavy meter and violent wars
